channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Nairobi Governor Mike Sonko Buys Limited-Edition Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold
item.description: Former Nairobi Governor Mike Sonko has just added a very rare gadget to his collection — the Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold. This phone is super exclusive, with only 500 units made worldwide, and Sonko has ...

item.title: Starmer faced criticism for appointing Mandelson despite Epstein connections
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing a political storm after new documents revealed he ignored warnings about Peter Mandelson’s links with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein before appoin...

item.title: UNILAG Lecturers Suspend Work Over Reduced Salaries for January and February 2026
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU) at the University of Lagos (UNILAG) don announce say dem go start indefinite strike over how the university pay their salary for January and February 20...

item.title: Pedro Neto apologises after incident with PSG ball boy during Chelsea's Champions League defeat
item.description: During Chelsea's Champions League match against PSG at the Parc des Princes, Pedro Neto was involved in an unusual incident with a ball boy in the closing stages. With Chelsea trailing 4-2 in injury t...

item.title: UN envoy visits Tanzania to assess national stability and government reforms
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has received a special message from the United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres, delivered by the UN Secretary-General’s Special Envoy, Mr Parfait Onanga-Anyang...

item.title: ABC staff vote overwhelmingly for protected industrial action amid stalled negotiations
item.description: The Australian Broadcasting Corporation (ABC) is on the brink of industrial action following a decisive vote by its staff in favour of protected action, including the possibility of strikes. The decis...

item.title: Man Charged with the Murder of Ian Huntley Appears at Court
item.description: Anthony Russell, 43, has appeared via videolink at Newton Aycliffe Magistrates' Court, charged with the murder of Ian Huntley, who was 52 at the time of his death. Huntley, infamous for the 2002 Soham...

item.title: Poilievre to visit U.S. to promote tariff-free trade for Canadian businesses
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre will travel to the United States this week to promote tariff-free trade for Canadian businesses and workers. His first stop is Michigan, where he will meet with le...

item.title: Tanzania Attributes Fourth-Quarter 2025 Diamond Production Decline to Seasonal and Operational Factors
item.description: Tanzania experienced a significant decline in diamond production during the last quarter of 2025, according to the government. The Bank of Tanzania reported that output dropped to 43,244 carats worth ...

item.title: After the Rain Returns Revitalized After Rewrites at NAC Ottawa
item.description: After its world premiere at Toronto’s Tarragon Theatre, 'After the Rain' faced criticism for an underdeveloped book and clunky musical numbers, despite having a promising premise. The production featu...

item.title: SA government criticises US ambassador for undiplomatic remarks
item.description: South Africa’s International Relations Minister Ronald Lamola has publicly criticised US Ambassador Brent Bozell for making remarks deemed 'undiplomatic' towards the South African government and Presi...

item.title: Uttarakhand Temples Restrict Entry to Non-Sanatanis
item.description: The Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee (BKTC) in Uttarakhand has recently approved a decision to restrict entry to 'non-Sanatanis', meaning those who do not follow Sanatan Dharma, in 47 temples unde...

item.title: Tunji-Ojo Congratulates Taiwo Oyedele on Appointment as Minister of State for Finance
item.description: The Minister of Interior, Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, don hail Taiwo Oyedele as e don get appointment as Minister of State for Finance by President Bola Tinubu. Tunji-Ojo talk say he dey happy say Oyedele, we...

item.title: Bomb threat received by UP Baguio prompts evacuation and class suspension; no explosive found
item.description: A bomb threat caused significant disruption at the University of the Philippines in Baguio City on March 11, 2026. The threat, which was reportedly sent to a school organization and specifically menti...

item.title: 2025 Health Report Reveals 31% of Nigerian Adolescents Are Underweight
item.description: A recent report from the Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare, titled the 2025 State of the Health of the Nation Report, don show say about one in three adolescents for Nigeria, exactly 31 pe...

item.title: Senators Urge Chief Justice to Expedite Sugar Act Court Cases to Aid Farmers
item.description: Senators from counties where sugarcane farming is a major economic activity have formally petitioned Chief Justice Martha Koome to fast-track court cases that are slowing the implementation of the Sug...

item.title: Makgopa secures late penalty as Orlando Pirates edge Richards Bay to regain top spot
item.description: Orlando Pirates secured a hard-fought 2-0 victory over Richards Bay FC at Orlando Stadium on Wednesday night, courtesy of an early goal by Oswin Appollis and a dramatic added-time penalty by Evidence ...

item.title: Barry Keoghan clarifies pronunciation of his surname amid fan debate
item.description: Irish actor Barry Keoghan addressed confusion over the pronunciation of his surname during an appearance on BBC Radio One's Breakfast Show. Host Greg James questioned him after listeners and friends s...

item.title: NTSB: Driver Distraction Preceded Fatal 2024 Ford BlueCruise Crashes
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) released documents on March 11, 2026, detailing two fatal crashes in 2024 involving Ford Mustang Mach-E vehicles using the BlueCruise hands-free driving...

item.title: Florida Woman Charged with Attempted Murder After Shooting at Rihanna's Los Angeles Home
item.description: A 35-year-old woman named Ivanna Lisette Ortiz from Orlando, Florida, has been formally charged following an incident where shots were fired at the Los Angeles home of pop superstar Rihanna on Sunday ...
